evidence_excerpt: At 7½ years she ambulates with a heel–toe gait and can walk up stairs; she is making progress with a picture exchange communication system, but has no speech, falls frequently, hypotonia, calf wasting, kyphosis, and signs of peripheral neuropathy (CMT1A).

quality_of_life.evidence_excerpt: Ongoing motor and neuropathic findings (calf wasting, ankle jerks difficult to elicit), crouched gait with frequent falls, dysmorphic and musculoskeletal issues (kyphosis, hip dislocations), and developmental delay without speech.

timeline_events.evidence_excerpt: She is making progress with a picture exchange communication system.
Bilateral congenital hip dislocations were diagnosed at the age of 7 years.

notes_on_uncertainty: The case report does not specify the exact date of definitive genetic diagnosis relative to clinical follow-up. Events prior to early childhood (e.g., surgery at 16 months, walking at 4½ years) likely pre-date diagnosis and were not included. Timing of listed post-index events is approximated as unknown due to insufficient detail on when diagnosis occurred.
